input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS targeting iOS and Android.

---

## 1. Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | clang, git, system headers |
| **.NET SDK (latest LTS or STS)** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | Runtime + CLI (`dotnet`) |
| **JDK 17** | Installed automatically by the MAUI workload, or manually from [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) | Android build toolchain |

## 2. Installation Steps (in order)

### Step 1 — Xcode
```bash
# After installing from the App Store:
sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
```

### Step 2 — .NET SDK
Download the macOS installer (`.pkg`) from [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download). Pick the **latest supported version** (check the [MAUI support policy](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/platform/support/policy/maui) to confirm which .NET versions are supported).

```bash
# Verify:
dotnet --version
```

### Step 3 — MAUI Workload
```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```
This pulls in everything: MAUI templates, Android SDK, Android NDK, Android emulator, and OpenJDK 17 (if not already present).

### Step 4 — Accept Android Licenses
```bash
# The Android SDK is installed under:
#   ~/Library/Android/sdk   (if installed via workload)
# Accept licenses:
~/Library/Android/sdk/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --licenses
```

## 3. Discover the Right Versions

```bash
# See which workloads are available and installed:
dotnet workload search
dotnet workload list

# See which .NET versions MAUI supports:
dotnet workload update    # pulls latest compatible manifests

# Check installed Android SDK components:
~/Library/Android/sdk/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --list
```

The official compatibility matrix lives at:
**[learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms)**

## 4. Verify Everything Works

```bash
# 1. Create a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# 2. Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios        # adjust TFM to your .NET version

# 3. Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# 4. Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SIMULATOR_UDID>
# Or simply use an IDE (see below)

# 5. Run on Android Emulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run

# 6. Full diagnostic check
dotnet workload list
dotnet --info
```

## 5. Recommended IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + [C# Dev Kit](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.csdevkit) + [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) | Free, officially supported |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** | **Discontinued** — use VS Code instead |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) (paid, has MAUI support) |

## 6. Common Gotchas

- **Xcode version mismatch**: Each .NET MAUI release requires a minimum Xcode version. Check the [release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/whats-new/).
- **Android emulator on Apple Silicon**: Use the **arm64-v8a** system images — x86_64 images won't run on M-series chips.
- **`dotnet workload restore`**: Run this after cloning an existing MAUI repo to install the exact workloads it needs.
- **Provisioning profiles**: For physical iOS devices, set up an Apple Developer account and configure signing in your `.csproj`.
